verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a secreted cysteine-rich secretory protein with an N-terminal signal peptide but no transmembrane domain or other stable membrane-anchoring mechanism. It is secreted by epididymal epithelium into the lumen and binds reversibly to sperm and egg surfaces. The loosely-bound population maintains equilibrium with the soluble pool and washes off; the tightly-bound population also lacks a covalent or integral-membrane anchor on the cell itself. CRISP1 is the recruited ligand, not a surface-anchored protein. Its surface presence on gametes is via transient non-covalent recruitment by the gamete surface, not via its own membrane anchor. This does not qualify as surface accessibility under the stated criterion.
